name: Simmons v. South Carolina
question: Does a state court’s refusal to instruct a jury in a sentencing trial that a defendant is ineligible for parole violate the Due Process Clause of the Fourteenth Amendment?
description: A case in which the Court held that a state court’s refusal to instruct a jury in a sentencing trial that a defendant is ineligible for parole violated the Due Process Clause of the Fourteenth Amendment.
